task: I’m drafting a reply to a vendor about a test failure, and the explanation they sent doesn’t seem to line up with the evidence. Can you help me write a short, professional note that asks for the missing details and keeps the discussion focused on what can actually be verified?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, professional version you can send:

> Thanks for the explanation. At this point, the proposed cause doesn’t fully line up with the test evidence we have. To move this forward, please send the specific details that support your conclusion, including the test conditions, affected units/lots, timing of the failure, and any data or observations that directly tie the failure to the stated cause.  
>   
> For now, let’s keep the discussion focused on verifiable facts so we can separate confirmed findings from assumptions.

If you want, I can also make it:
- firmer,
- more diplomatic, or
- more technical / test-report style.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Short, clean, and it keeps them on evidence instead of hand-waving. The part about verifiable facts is the right tone — not accusatory, just controlled.

I’d probably use it as-is.
